name: Braised Pork Chops with Prunes and Apricots - Bon Appetit
recipeIngredient: 8 Center-cut pork chops (about 1/2 inch thick), trimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Season pork with salt and pepper.
2. Place flour in a pie dish. Coat the pork with flour, shaking off excess.
3. Heat oil in a heavy large Dutch oven over high heat.
4. Add half of the pork chops; cook until brown, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer to paper towels; drain.
5. Repeat with remaining pork chops. Drain all but 2 tablespoons oil from the Dutch oven.
6. Add sliced onions to the Dutch oven and saute until light brown, about 5 minutes.
7. Place pork chops over onions. Add broth and wine. Bring to a simmer.
8. Reduce heat to medium-low. Cover; cook for 15 minutes.
9. Add fruit. Cover; simmer until pork is very tender, about 45 minutes.
- (Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover, chill. Rewarm over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, before continuing.)
10. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pork to a platter. Using the same spoon, arrange onions and fruit around pork.
11. Tent with foil. Boil cooking liquid until thickened slightly, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes.
12. Season with salt and pepper. Spoon over pork.
13. Top with parsley.
